title: Sage category to normalized category-expression ledger
normalization_contract: A mapping consists of a named normalized base category plus an ordered finite list of classifier applications.
A classifier is available when its least host is reachable from the current formal cone by canonical structural paths and registered finite-limit lifts.
Classifier edges are never reversed; pullback formation is an inference rule on a cospan.
Generated pullback projections and their composites are not separately authored mapping data.
Named targets are aliases of normalized expressions, not duplicate semantic nodes.
Finite means literal finiteness of the underlying set; FiniteRank is distinct.
Additive and multiplicative are operation roles in one magma theory; route/port selectors disambiguate multiple operations.
Framework/test classes receive explicit dispositions rather than artificial mathematical targets.

normalized_graph_input.status: audited as a current visual/design source; ledger records required additions and corrections

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — keep as implicit expression unless reused
audit.note: Sage's FiniteDimensional is module-relative; normalize as finite rank, not finite underlying set.

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — keep as implicit expression unless reused
audit.note: FiniteDimensional is normalized to the module-relative FiniteRank classifier.

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — keep as implicit expression unless reused
no — keep as implicit expression unless reused
no — keep as implicit expression unless reused

audit.note: This is the essential image/family of the monoid-algebra construction R[−], not an axiom refinement of arbitrary algebras.
AbelianVariety packages the compatible commutative group-scheme structure plus the standard smooth/proper/geometrically connected conditions.
Additive is a role/presentation classifier on the single magma theory.
Sage's source explicitly defines AlgebraModules(A) as modules over the fixed commutative algebra A.
Sage's unqualified Algebras category is associative and unital.

normalized.parameter_constraints: C : additive/abelian category; degree convention parameter

audit.note: Standard functor/diagram category with differential and square-zero relation.
The conventional mathematical name is AbelianGroups; the expanded expression is Groups.Additive.Commutative.
Commutativity is a condition on the ambient algebra parameter, not an additional classifier on its ideals.
Commutativity constrains the ambient ring parameter; it is not an additional property of an ideal object.
Intended objects are finitely generated groups with a distinguished finite simple-reflection system and its induced reflection data.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: Sage explicitly states that no general mathematical theory currently unifies complex reflection groups and generalized Coxeter groups; the class exists to factor common code. Retain a descriptive host rather than pretending this is a standard species.

audit.note: Group algebras whose distinguished group input is a Coxeter group; retain as a standard construction specialization.
CWStructure is chosen cellular filtration/attaching data, with the usual topology and closure-finiteness/weak-topology laws.
The valuation is part of the usable structure, not merely existence of some valuation.
DiscreteValuation includes the distinguished valuation/uniformizer structure or the equivalent local-DVR property; precise classifier character must be fixed.
Transparent exceptional host for a carrier with distinguished additive and multiplicative binary operations; synthetically Magmas.Additive ×_Sets Magmas.Multiplicative.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: No universally dominant short noun exists; use a transparent descriptive name because this pullback is the least host of distributivity.

audit.note: Division is the ring-relative condition that every nonzero multiplicative element is invertible.
Sage's Domains permits the noncommutative ring notion determined by its NoZeroDivisors axiom.

normalized.parameter_constraints: finite field F_q, function ring F_q[T], field K, and base morphism γ

audit.note: The category is indexed by the fixed base morphism γ and consists of Drinfeld-module homomorphisms satisfying the usual Ore-polynomial conditions.
Enumerated carries an enumeration interface/witness, not merely countability.

audit.exception_or_ambiguity: Facade is a Sage implementation/delegation flag, not a mathematical predicate or structure. Record as backend metadata, not a normalized classifier.

audit.base_in_current_dot: present but defining label needs correction

audit.note: Sage permits R=ZZ; its FiniteDimensional axiom therefore means finite rank relative to the module structure, not vector-space dimension.

audit.note: FinitelyGenerated is relative to the lambda-bracket algebra structure.

normalized.parameter_constraints: Sage leaves the constant field implicit

audit.note: Sage declares an unparameterized subcategory of Fields. Keep FunctionFields as the standard named target; later refine the classifier by constant field/transcendence-degree conventions.
GAction is chosen action structure; the target is the standard category of G-sets.

audit.note: Essential image/family of the group-algebra construction R[−].

normalized.parameter_constraints: G : Sets (Sage usually supplies a group)

audit.note: Best interpretation: the fiber of an underlying-set/object-set functor from groupoids over the specified G.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: Sage's source explicitly marks the definition and relation with the standard notion of groupoid as FIXME; its sole parent is Sets. Do not silently map it to the full category Groupoids.

audit.note: Groups is a standard named category; its synthetic definition may be Monoids.Inverse.

normalized.parameter_constraints: R : CommutativeRings; object-dependent level/anemic Hecke action

audit.note: Sage defines an R-module with distinguished basis and action of the anemic Hecke algebra determined by the object's level.
Values of the hom bifunctor, organized as the appropriate category of hom-objects/sets.
KählerPackage is enabled only after WithBasis, Graded, and FiniteRank have been imposed; it abbreviates Poincaré duality, hard Lefschetz, and Hodge–Riemann relations.

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — keep as implicit expression unless reused

audit.note: Use the mathematical distributive-lattice predicate. Sage's current implementation route via Trim and ChainGraded is not the normalized definition.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: Upstream implementation chain does not literally express the docstring's distributive law; normalize by intention.

audit.note: Lattice means every pair has a meet and join; this category hosts further lattice classifiers.
Normalized Modules(R) denotes left R-modules; Sage's LeftModules is an explicit spelling of that standard category.

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — keep as implicit expression unless reused

audit.note: A group object compatible with smooth/analytic manifold structure; retain explicitly rather than forcing a premature single pullback presentation.
Retain as the standard affine/loop-crystal category rather than force a premature classifier decomposition.
Transparent exceptional host for a carrier with distinguished additive and multiplicative binary operations; synthetically Magmas.Additive ×_Sets Magmas.Multiplicative.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: No universally dominant short noun exists; use a transparent descriptive name because this pullback is the least host of distributivity.

audit.note: Normalized Algebras(R) means an R-module with a bilinear product, with no associativity or unit imposed.
Complex atlas plus holomorphic transition compatibility; a standard structured refinement.
Retain as a standard named host; smooth/PL/complex refinements are classifiers.
Interpreted as algebras isomorphic to a full matrix algebra (possibly with chosen matrix presentation); Sage's category is intentionally broad and dimension-unspecified.
Metric is chosen structure whose induced topology supplies the projection to TopologicalSpaces.
Sage's source intends modular abelian varieties over a field; its recorded supercategory Sets is explicitly marked FIXME.

Map to the ordinary category of left R-modules when R is commutative (the intended/common Sage use). Sage's source models symmetric (R,R)-bimodules and explicitly warns that noncommutative semantics are unsettled.
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: For noncommutative R, Sage Modules(R) is not a reliable synonym for normalized left Modules(R); the adapter must inspect/limit the parameter or map to the appropriate symmetric-bimodule refinement.

audit.note: WithBasis is chosen basis data.
Monoids is a standard named category; its synthetic definition may be Semigroups.Unital.
Sage defines Noetherian rings as commutative rings in which every ideal is finitely generated.
A number field is a finite field extension of Q; the parameter Q is part of the classifier.

audit.base_in_current_dot: compatibility-only; do not add as ordinary mathematical node
audit.exception_or_ambiguity: Sage's Objects() contains every backend object and has no honest small mathematical analogue. Do not identify it with Cat.

normalized.parameter_constraints: R : CommutativeRings; σ endomorphism and/or σ-derivation δ

audit.note: Sage places OreModules(R, twist) over Modules(R); the extra datum is the compatible Ore-semilinear operator/action.
A monoid equipped with a partial order compatible with multiplication.
A permutation group is a group with a chosen faithful action/embedding into a symmetric group.
Pointed is chosen basepoint data.

normalized.parameter_constraints: R typically ZZ, QQ, RR, or an ordered coefficient ring

audit.note: Sage means polyhedral subsets over a coefficient ring; normalize the class spelling PolyhedralSets to the standard category name Polyhedra.
Standard named category; synthetically it may be Sets.PartialOrder.

normalized.parameter_constraints: R : CommutativeRings/Fields; quantum-group/root datum parameters are part of the representation structure

audit.note: Sage's immediate supercategory is Modules(R); the added structure is the quantum-group action.
Sage intends fraction fields of integral domains with numerator/denominator presentation. The normalized standard name is FractionFields.

audit.recommend_explicit_target: no — use the Modules family at Rᵒᵖ
audit.note: Right R-modules are left modules over the opposite ring; no parallel primitive module species is needed.
Category of ideals in a fixed ring; naturally a subobject/ideal construction rather than a property of arbitrary objects.
Fundamental named node even if synthetically definable.
Standard category of associative nonunital rings; normalized graph may define it synthetically from the two-operation host.
Sage intends Jacobians attached to curves or function fields over K; base_curve is part of the usable presentation.
Fundamental standard category and a classifier host; retain explicitly.
Semigroups is a standard named category; its synthetic definition may be Magmas.Associative.
Graded is a chosen grading structure at the Sets host.
A distinct category with partial maps as morphisms; it is not a subcategory of Sets.
Sage places these under finite generalized Coxeter groups; Shephard is the standard additional condition.
Standard combinatorial category; retain explicitly because its clean categorical presentation is not just a single inherited classifier.
Standard presheaf/functor category Δ^op → Sets.

normalized_classifier_vocabulary.audit_note: Abelian-category property; helper class itself is not a category node.
Commutative group-scheme structure together with the standard abelian-variety hypotheses.
Marks the operation as additive; does not introduce a second operation by itself.
Affine Weyl type.
Almost-complex structure.
Analytic regularity.
Top and bottom elements.
Cellular-algebra package.
All maximal chains have the same length.
Ordinary cocommutativity.
Route-sensitive when a base has more than one magma port.
Topological compactness.
Completeness of the canonical metric/uniform structure; valued rings and fields reach this host through their valuation metric.
Complex atlas with holomorphic transition maps.
Congruence-uniform lattice.
Cell decomposition and attaching data.
Differentiable regularity.
The normalized classifier must specify whether a valuation/uniformizer is chosen or merely exists.
Lattice distributive laws; distinct classifier from ring-like distributivity.
Distributivity between the two chosen magma operations.
Nonzero multiplicative invertibility in a ring; not plain Magmas.Inverse.
Enumeration interface/witness, stronger and more computational than countability.
Extremal lattice.
Equivalent to a chosen embedding into a symmetric group.
Chosen filtration by compatible submodules; transported to algebraic structures.
Finite underlying carrier, literally pulled back from Sets.
Finite degree over Q, including the specified Q-algebra structure.
Must be indexed by the underlying algebraic signature/route, e.g. magma or lambda-bracket algebra.
Generation is taken under the lambda-bracket algebra operations.
Finite module presentation.
Module-relative finite rank; distinct from Sets.Finite.
Chosen realization as the fraction field of an integral domain, supporting numerator/denominator.
Precise constant-field and transcendence conventions remain to be parameterized.
Chosen action of a fixed group G.
Chosen grading; compatibility with additional operations is enforced in the transported structured category.
Action of the object-dependent anemic Hecke algebra.
Green H-relation is trivial.
x·x=x; on semigroups the resulting standard named category is Bands.
Inverse operation and laws.
Irreducibility of the distinguished simple-reflection system.
Green J-relation is trivial.
Poincaré duality, hard Lefschetz, and Hodge–Riemann relations.
Existence of binary meets and joins; may be presented with chosen operations canonically determined by the order.
Green L-relation is trivial.
Being presented/isomorphic as a full matrix algebra; Sage leaves size unspecified.
Metric inducing the displayed topology.
Use the actual predicate name; Domains is the conventional named result.
Compatible action of the Ore polynomial ring determined by σ and δ.
Chosen basepoint.
Pointed object of the category of simplicial sets.
Action of the relevant quantum group; root datum and deformation parameters must be explicit.
Crystal-theoretic regularity.
Green R-relation is trivial.
Semidistributive lattice.
Smooth regularity.
Stone lattice.
Stratification compatible with the grading.
Z/2-graded module structure; transported to algebras, Hopf algebras, and Lie conformal algebras.
Super-crystal structure is not the same classifier as Z/2-graded modules.
Super sign-rule cocommutativity.
Graded-commutativity with Koszul sign.
Trim lattice.
Includes a distinguished unit and its laws.
Well-generated complex reflection group.
Chosen basis data; transports to any category with a canonical underlying module.

sage_axiom_crosswalk.mapping_action: merge with one magma axiom; route additive
sage_axiom_crosswalk.audit_note: Sage duplicates the magma law in an additive namespace. Normalize to Associative applied to the additive operation port.

sage_axiom_crosswalk.mapping_action: merge with one magma axiom; route additive
sage_axiom_crosswalk.audit_note: Normalize to the ordinary Commutative classifier on the additive operation port.

sage_axiom_crosswalk.mapping_action: merge with one magma axiom; route additive
sage_axiom_crosswalk.audit_note: Normalize to Inverse on the additive unital-magma port.

sage_axiom_crosswalk.mapping_action: merge with one magma axiom; route additive
sage_axiom_crosswalk.audit_note: Normalize to Unital on the additive operation port.

Analytic manifold regularity; keep qualified by the manifold host.

Ordinary associativity of the selected magma operation.
Existence of top and bottom elements; these are unique when they exist.
Cellular algebra package; exact choice-vs-property semantics should follow the intended cell datum.

Ordinary cocommutativity.
The registry's declaring fixture is not authoritative. Use the least mathematical host Magmas and require an operation-port selector when the base has more than one magma structure.
Topological compactness.
Metric completeness. Valued rings/fields use their valuation-metric port.
Standard finite-lattice property.

Sage uses the unqualified word in several unrelated theories (topological spaces, graphs/CW objects, graded Hopf algebras, filtered objects). Do not introduce one global Connected classifier; qualify each occurrence by host.
Differentiable manifold regularity.
Lattice distributivity and ring-style distributivity are different classifiers. The latter is hosted on MagmasWithTwoOperations.
Nonzero multiplicative elements are invertible, with the ring-specific zero convention.
Endsets are diagonal Hom objects; algebraic structure on an endomorphism object is recorded by the relevant construction/functors, not a global axiom.
An explicit enumeration interface/structure, not mere countability.
Standard extremal-lattice property.
Sage delegation/facade mechanism.
Literal finiteness of the underlying set whenever transported.
Normalize module-relative finite dimensionality to FiniteRank; never to Sets.Finite.
Finite generation in the Lambda-bracket signature.
Finite generation by the selected magma operation.
Module finite presentation.
Green H-relation is trivial.

Inverse operation/law on a unital magma.
Indecomposability of the simple-reflection system.
Green J-relation is a semigroup notion; normalize at Semigroups.
Green L-relation is trivial.
Nilpotent Lie algebra.
Ring-specific no-zero-divisors condition.

sage_axiom_crosswalk.audit_note: A pointed simplicial set is a pointed object of the simplicial-set category, not merely a simplicial set whose one underlying carrier is pointed.

Standard semidistributive-lattice property.
Smooth manifold regularity.
Stone-lattice property.
Stratification of a graded Lie algebra.
Cocommutativity with the super sign rule.
Commutativity with the super sign rule.
Standard trim-lattice property.
The registry fixture is not authoritative; apply to the selected magma-operation port.
Well-generated complex reflection group.
Distinguished basis data; not an invariant property.
Doctest-only registry token.
Doctest-only registry token.

sage_functorial_construction_crosswalk.audit_note: Treat as a genuine functorial construction. Its named values include MonoidAlgebras(R), GroupAlgebras(R), and CoxeterGroupAlgebras(R); do not confuse it with the category family Algebras(R).
Generated category of product objects; semantic structure comes from products/monoidal structure, not an axiom token.
Record the dual object and evaluation/coevaluation data where appropriate.
In the normalized graph, model filtration as chosen structure at the least suitable host and transport it by pullback.
Normalize to the Graded classifier declared at Sets and transported to structured categories.
Map values to Homsets(C) or the appropriate hom bifunctor output.
Represent categorically; do not create a property axiom.
Adds metric structure, with induced/compatible topology where TopologicalSpaces is used as the named base.
Represent via epimorphism/coslice-style data or the selected quotient construction.
Presentation layer, not a mathematical invariant.
Category of monomorphisms/subobjects; not an axiom on objects.
Composite of subobject and quotient constructions.
Normalize as the Super classifier/structure at Modules(R), then transport to algebraic descendants.
Functorial construction governed by monoidal structure.
Adds topology to an underlying set or compatible structured object.
Presentation/witness layer; keep outside intrinsic classifier vocabulary.

nonpublic_source_class_dispositions.audit_note: The example category has an ordinary mathematical meaning: idempotent semigroups, conventionally bands.
Implementation class for Sage's Algebras functorial construction; no separate normalized category node.
Implementation class; concrete construction values are normalized recursively.
Python framework base class, not an object of the normalized mathematical graph.
Implementation base for parameterized Sage categories; parameterization is recorded in concrete normalized families.
Each instance maps recursively to the normalized finite intersection/pullback of its factors; the helper class itself is not a category node.
Singleton-allocation implementation base; no mathematical semantics.
The helper class is not itself the category of abelian categories; concrete claims should map to the Abelian classifier on category objects.
Implementation base for concrete ideal categories, which are mapped individually.
Implementation base for categories internal to an ambient parent; concrete categories supply the mathematics.
Implementation base for module-like categories; concrete module categories are mapped individually.
Implementation base for parameterized categories over a base object.
Implementation base for categories over a base ring.
Technical category wrapper for elements; not a stable mathematical category species.
Generic implementation of classifier refinement; concrete C.A instances are normalized by the axiom rule.
Parameterized implementation helper for classifier refinements.
Singleton implementation helper for classifier refinements.
Framework base for construction result categories; concrete construction values map via the construction crosswalk.
Framework base for construction result categories.
Framework base for base-changing construction result categories.
Implementation class for the dual-object construction.
Implementation class for the filtered-object construction; no additional semantic node.
Helper implementing the already-mapped public graded Lie-conformal category.
Implementation class for graded modules.
Implementation class for C ↦ Homsets(C).
Specialized implementation class for a value of the Homsets construction.
Implementation class for closure under isomorphic objects; normalize as the corresponding categorical construction.
Implementation class for the metric refinement/construction.
Implementation class for quotient objects.
Category of realizations/presentations of a fixed parent; retain as presentation-layer construction.
Implementation class for realizations.
Runtime implementation class of the ordinary slice-like family of schemes over S.
Implementation class for super/signed tensor products.
Implementation class for subobjects; normalized as a category of monomorphisms/suitable slice construction.
Implementation class for the composite subquotient construction.
Implementation class for the super/Z₂-graded module construction.
Implementation class for tensor-product objects.

Implementation class for attaching a realization/presentation layer.

audit_exceptions.finding: Universal backend object fallback, not a standard small mathematical category.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Keep AmbientObjects only in the Sage compatibility layer; do not conflate it with Cat or use it as an ordinary mathematical classifier host.
Map the mathematical category to Sets and retain Facade as backend metadata; no normalized Facade classifier.

audit_exceptions.topic: One magma theory, two operation roles
audit_exceptions.finding: Sage duplicates the magma axiom tower for additive spelling.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Use one Magmas host; Additive is a role/presentation classifier. Preserve route/port data when an object has two operations.

audit_exceptions.finding: Distributivity requires simultaneous access to two independently selected magma structures.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Add the explicit named pullback host MagmasWithTwoOperations := Magmas.Additive ×_Sets Magmas.Multiplicative and attach Distributive@TwoOperations there.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage describes compatible left/right actions over a commutative ring and warns that noncommutative semantics are fuzzy; it also has separate LeftModules, RightModules, and Bimodules.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Let normalized Modules(R) mean left R-modules. Map Sage LeftModules(R) to Modules(R), RightModules(R) to Modules(Rᵒᵖ), and Bimodules(R,S) to the standard bimodule family. Map Sage Modules(R) directly only in its reliable commutative/symmetric regime; review noncommutative instances.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage UnitalAlgebras means unital possibly nonassociative algebras, whereas Sage Algebras means associative unital algebras.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Map UnitalAlgebras to Algebras(R).Unital and Algebras to Algebras(R).Associative.Unital. Rename the current DOT node that incorrectly uses UnitalAlgebras for the latter.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage applies FiniteDimensional over general base rings, including ZZ.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Normalize as FiniteRank on the underlying module; never as Sets.Finite and never silently as field-vector-space dimension.

audit_exceptions.finding: The docstring intends distributive lattices, while the current implementation returns Trim().ChainGraded().
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Use LatticePosets.Distributive@Lattice as the definition; record the Sage implementation route as a backend discrepancy.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage explicitly says there is no general theory unifying the two branches; the category factors common code around distinguished simple reflections.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Retain a descriptive explicit host GroupsWithSimpleReflections, then refine to ComplexReflection or GeneralizedCoxeter.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage's source marks its definition and link with the standard notion of groupoid as FIXME and parameterizes it by an underlying set G.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Provisional target GroupoidsOn(G), interpreted as an appropriate fiber over G; require source/use-site confirmation before claiming exactness.

audit_exceptions.finding: Division-ring invertibility concerns nonzero multiplicative elements in a ring, not an unqualified Magmas.Inverse classifier.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Attach Division at Rings; define DivisionRings := Rings.Division and Fields := DivisionRings.Commutative.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage source intends modular abelian varieties over a field but reports Sets as supercategory with an explicit FIXME.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Map to AbelianVarieties(K).Modular, K a field.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage records an unparameterized subcategory of Fields and leaves constant-field conventions implicit.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Use the named target Fields.FunctionField now; later parameterize constant field and transcendence-degree conventions when the definitions are fixed.

audit_exceptions.finding: These names restrict or re-present parameters rather than add object-level structure.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Map to Modules(A), AlgebraIdeals(A), or RingIdeals(R) with parameter constraints; do not mint new classifiers.

audit_exceptions.finding: The category is not merely graded finite-rank algebra; it requires Poincaré duality, hard Lefschetz, and Hodge–Riemann relations.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Use AssociativeUnitalAlgebras(R).WithBasis.Graded.FiniteRank.KählerPackage and retain KählerAlgebras as the conventional specialist name.

audit_exceptions.finding: Sage registers one unqualified Connected name but uses it in unrelated theories.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Split by least host (for example topological connectedness, graph connectedness, and connected grading); never create one global Connected classifier.

audit_exceptions.topic: Pointed simplicial sets versus pointed sets
audit_exceptions.finding: These are related but not the same classifier application.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Keep Sets.Pointed for pointed sets and PointedObject on SimplicialSets for pointed simplicial sets.

audit_exceptions.finding: Framework classes and test fixtures must not be mistaken for independent mathematical categories.
audit_exceptions.normalized_resolution: Give every class an explicit disposition: construction implementation, recursive normalization rule, test exclusion, or—in the example IdempotentSemigroups case—the mathematical target Bands.
